message:
test(pager): in-process multi-thread Waiter-path stress for group commit

Cross-process swarm_multiprocess exercises the Flusher path almost
exclusively because each process owns its GroupCommitQueue
(the consolidator is process-local). 04812db3 fixed the ghost-commit
bug where merged prepared batches retained intermediate db_size_if_commit
markers in their frame bytes; the existing unit test verifies the merge
function in isolation. This adds end-to-end coverage under contention.

8 worker threads each commit 50 transactions against a single shared
SimplePager + GroupCommitQueue via commit_wal_group_commit. A custom
WAL backend captures, for each append_prepared_frames call:
- meta_commit_count (frames with meta.db_size_if_commit != 0)
- byte_commit_count (4-byte db_size_if_commit field read from frame_bytes[4..8])
- frame_count

Invariants asserted (all catching the pre-04812db3 ghost-commit shape):
- meta_commits <= 1 per batch
- byte_commits <= 1 per batch
- meta_commits == byte_commits per batch
- at least one batch had >2 frames (proving Waiter-path merging happened)
- total frames recorded == total frames submitted (800 for 8x50x2)

The dry-run count shows 174 of 400 txns were merged into shared epochs
in a single local run (8 threads, 50 iters), so the Waiter path is
exercised heavily.

bd-3wop3.8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
